steps.title: Identify what would support the researchers' claim
steps.content: The researchers claim archaeologists won't be replaced despite a model being equally accurate at categorization. To support this claim directly, we need evidence that archaeologists have irreplaceable value beyond just categorizing pottery—work that a computer model cannot do.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the claim
steps.content:
Choice C — A survey of archaeologists showed that categorizing pottery fragments limits the amount of time they can dedicate to other important tasks that only human experts can do. shows that categorization limits archaeologists' time for tasks only human experts can do.

If a computer model handles categorization, archaeologists can focus on that uniquely human work, making replacement unlikely.

The other three choices either show why the model would be preferable, make the accuracy comparison irrelevant, or fail to explain why replacement won't happen.

That matches Choice C — A survey of archaeologists showed that categorizing pottery fragments limits the amount of time they can dedicate to other important tasks that only human experts can do..
